# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Python data processing application. Start from a full Ubuntu base image and install Python, pip, curl, wget, git, vim, and other useful development tools. This gives us flexibility for debugging and running various scripts in production.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Python runtime as the parent image
FROM ubuntu:latest

# Set environment variables
ENV PYTHON_VERSION 3.8.5

# Install tools needed for python development
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y software-properties-common curl wget git vim && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Adds the deadsnakes PPA to your sources list and then runs apt-get update. This will add Python 3.8 to your system’s repositories.
RUN add-apt-repository ppa:deadsnakes/ppa && \
    apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y python${PYTHON_VERSION} && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
    
# Install pip for python3.8
RUN curl https://bootstrap.pypa.io/pip/3.8/get-pip.py -o get-pip.py && \
    python${PYTHON_VERSION} get-pip.py && \
    rm -rf get-pip.py

# Set the working directory in the container to /app
WORKDIR /app
